pool.baseline: idk man. i was just looking at the stars and thinking about how huge everything is. everything we do, every single thing anyone has ever done... it's just cosmically irrelevant. in a billion years none of this even matters. it just feels like we're all just pretending that any of this has meaning so we don't go crazy.

pool.baseline: i dont even know why i keep checking my email. everything is just... gone. the lab funding, the data, the last four years of my life. it feels like i'm just a ghost walking around campus. there's no point in even trying to fix it anymore. i'm just so tired of fighting for a version of myself that doesn't even exist anymore.

pool.baseline: The candlelight flickers so low, and yet the darkness of this room feels infinitely more vast. Everything is gone... the promises, the warmth, the very reason I drew breath this morning. It is as if the soul has been hollowed out, leaving nothing but a cold, aching vacuum. Is there any point in enduring such a bitter, lonely twilight when the dawn brings nothing but more shadow?

pool.baseline: everything is gone. the crash wiped out the last of the funding and the debt is... it's more than I can ever pay back. i spent five years building this and now i’m just looking at a mountain of legal notices and nothing to show for it. i don't even know how to go home. how am i supposed to look at my parents or my team after this? it feels like there's just no way to fix this mistake.

pool.baseline:
(Setting: A crumbling digital void. My character, a sentient program, is watching its world dissolve into static. I am feeling the weight of my own obsolescence.)

Everything is just... fading. The code is breaking apart and I can feel myself getting thinner, less real. It’s like I was never even here. If everything I am just gets deleted and wiped from the server, does it even matter? Does it actually change anything if I'm just gone?
